finding: Frozen runner can continue surfacing a terminal imported task (TASK-SYNC-0001) after source-first routing correctly excludes it, and runner-sync admission can then deadlock because the completed sync task is terminal while the only frozen queue head is the ghost task.
expectedBehavior: Release runner artifacts must contain the same terminal-task exclusion logic as source, closedAt/closedByActor tasks must not appear in fallback open queues, and runner-sync broker/admission should expose a first-layer recovery path when a release/source split blocks normal build.
evidenceOrRepro: After commit 4d740e05c48b712154c58ed93292a2044d711d75, `node atm.dev.mjs next --prompt <governance batch prompt> --json` returned `openTaskCount: 0` / `ATM_NEXT_TASK_SCOPE_NOT_FOUND`, while `node atm.mjs next --prompt <same prompt> --json` returned TASK-SYNC-0001 with status blocked plus closedAt=2026-07-17T05:11:07.307Z. `npm run build` then failed with ATM_RUNNER_SYNC_QUEUE_HEAD_REQUIRED, and `broker runner-sync enqueue --task ATM-FRAMEWORK-TEMP-codex-plan-series-skill` failed with ATM_RUNNER_SYNC_ENQUEUE_TASK_INVALID because that task is terminal.
followUp: Closed by ATM-GOV-0230: `runner-sync-terminal-task-parity.test.ts` proves terminal ghost queue heads are removed and waiting live work advances; `runner-sync-framework-temp-hotfix.test.ts` keeps live framework temp owners active while ordinary missing/terminal owners remain invalid; retained release artifacts must be synced after the task close so frozen `node atm.mjs` projects the same behavior.
